name: praxiskit
description: Typed A→B transforms for software development workflows: turn product intent into governed agent execution and final acceptance review through composable skills and recipes.

interface.shortDescription: Typed transform pipelines for agentic software work
interface.longDescription: PraxisKit v3 turns ideas into right-sized plans, coordinated agent execution, and final result reviews via typed A→B transforms (skills) and recipes (named chains). See RECIPES.md for how to compose them.
defaultPrompt: For toy projects or scripts, use the light recipe: invoke $seed-to-task-graph then $task-graph-to-batch.

For most features, use the standard recipe: invoke $seed-to-idea, then $idea-to-prd, then $prd-to-task-graph. When the seed is sparse (1-2 sentences), gate eagerly via host-native multi-question UI — do not draft and infer.
To resume a loop, use $next-iteration. Pass budget=N or cycles=N for a bounded multi-step run.
When PraxisKit offers a host-native choice prompt, use it to continue planning or stop instead of asking me to type the next slash command.
Execute only when I explicitly authorize it: $batch-to-build refuses without authorization.
Close the loop with $review-and-accept (review packet + decision + archive).
